results.taskDescription: Fetch 20 posts; output 'highest score' AND 'most comments' as two separate sections. Tests synthesis of multiple slices from one observation.
Fetch top 15 HN posts and write a paragraph-style summary categorizing them by topic. Tests substantive synthesis from observation data.
results.output:
# Today on Hacker News

## Technology & Hardware Innovation
The technology landscape is buzzing with hardware innovations and developer tools. Valve has made a significant move by releasing their Steam Controller CAD files under Creative Commons license, allowing enthusiasts to modify or build upon this gaming peripheral design. In the software realm, Supabase users are exploring new authentication options through Clerk, while Google introduces an advanced fraud defense system as the next evolution of reCAPTCHA. The open-source community also saw a new email builder tool emerge as an alternative to commercial solutions like Beefree and Unlayer.

## Software Development & Productivity
Developer productivity remains a hot topic on Hacker News this week. A compelling article titled "The bottleneck was never the code" challenges conventional wisdom about software development bottlenecks, suggesting that human factors often play a more significant role than technical limitations. The discussion around appearing productive in the workplace adds another layer to this conversation, offering insights into workplace dynamics and performance perception.

## AI & Machine Learning
The field of artificial intelligence continues to evolve rapidly. A new theory paper on deep learning has sparked interest among researchers, while developments in diffusion models are pushing the boundaries of machine learning applications. These advancements highlight the ongoing progress in understanding neural networks and their practical implementations.

## Other Notable Stories
This week's coverage extends beyond technology into broader cultural and business spheres. The passing of Ted Turner marks a significant moment in media history, while discussions about smartphone camera quality reflect evolving consumer expectations in mobile photography. Additionally, an interesting project showcasing a custom-built robot dog demonstrates the intersection of robotics and DIY innovation.

The diverse range of topics covered this week reflects the broad scope of technological discourse on Hacker News, from hardware design to software development practices, AI research, and cultural impact stories that shape our digital world.
